title: Editor missing features: line numbers, find/replace, auto-close brackets, completion positioning
analysis:
This task addresses 6 editor feature gaps in the CodeMirror-based editor component.

E-01 (line numbers): The Editor.tsx extensions array (lines 60-125) does not include lineNumbers(). The lineNumbers extension is in @codemirror/view which is already a dependency.

E-02 (find/replace): @codemirror/search is NOT currently installed — needs npm install. The search() extension and searchKeymap need to be added to Editor.tsx.

E-03 (closeBrackets): The reifyLanguage.ts (line 24) defines closeBrackets language data with brackets config, but the closeBrackets() extension from @codemirror/autocomplete (already a dependency) is not loaded in Editor.tsx. Without the extension, the language data has no effect.

E-11 (completion from position): In completions.ts line 112, `from: pos` uses cursor position directly. When cursor is mid-word (e.g. typing 'wid|'), accepting a completion inserts the full label starting at cursor, duplicating the already-typed prefix. Fix: scan backward from pos to find the word start (alphanumeric/underscore characters) and use that as `from`.

E-12 (cross-file goto-definition): In gotoDefinition.ts lines 99-112, the mousedown handler only navigates when location.uri matches the current file. For cross-file definitions, the handler silently does nothing. Fix: add an onNavigate callback parameter that Editor.tsx can use to load the target file via bridge.openFile and open it in the editorStore.

E-14 (hover tooltip styling): The hover.ts create() function (line 75) sets className 'cm-lsp-hover' and some inline padding/width styles, but there's no background color, border, or shadow. The tooltip appears as invisible text floating over the editor. Fix: add proper CSS rules for .cm-lsp-hover in Editor.module.css.

Existing test patterns: Editor.test.tsx renders full Editor with mocked Tauri APIs and inspects CM6 DOM. Module-level tests (completions.test.ts, gotoDefinition.test.ts, hover.test.ts) mock invoke and test functions in isolation.

**REVIEW FIX (steps 13-14):** Step 10 only modified gotoDefinition.ts to accept the onNavigate callback but never wired it in Editor.tsx. The call site at Editor.tsx:73 remains `reifyGotoDefinition(() => currentUri)` with no second argument. The entire cross-file goto-definition feature (E-12) is dead code in production. Steps 13-14 add the missing integration: an Editor.test.tsx integration test and the actual wiring in Editor.tsx.

prerequisites.description: Install @codemirror/search package: add to gui/package.json dependencies and run npm install to generate lockfile entry. This is needed for E-02 find/replace support.

steps.description: E-01 test: Add test in Editor.test.tsx in a new 'Editor extensions' describe block asserting that the rendered editor contains a .cm-lineNumbers element (CodeMirror renders a gutter with this class when lineNumbers() extension is loaded).
E-01 impl: In Editor.tsx, import { lineNumbers } from '@codemirror/view' (add to existing import on line 3) and add lineNumbers() to the extensions array before bracketMatching().

E-02 test: Add test in Editor.test.tsx asserting that after dispatching a Ctrl+F keydown event on the editor's contentDOM, a .cm-search element appears in the editor container (CodeMirror's search panel).

E-02 impl: In Editor.tsx, import { search } from '@codemirror/search' and import { searchKeymap } from '@codemirror/search'. Add search() to the extensions array and add ...searchKeymap to the keymap.of() array.

E-03 test: Add test in Editor.test.tsx. Set up editor with an empty document, type '{' by dispatching view.dispatch({changes: {from: 0, insert: '{'}}). This alone won't trigger closeBrackets (it operates via keyboard input handling). Instead, verify the extension is loaded by checking that the closeBrackets extension is present: use a simulated input event or check that the extension configuration includes closeBrackets. Alternatively, verify via the editor state's facet that closeBrackets is active. Simplest approach: fire a keyboard event for '{' key and check that doc contains '{}'.

E-03 impl: In Editor.tsx, import { closeBrackets, closeBracketsKeymap } from '@codemirror/autocomplete' (extend existing import on line 6). Add closeBrackets() to the extensions array after bracketMatching(). Add ...closeBracketsKeymap to the keymap.of() array before ...defaultKeymap.

E-11 test: Add test in completions.test.ts: 'completion result.from scans backward to word start'. Create a context mock where pos=3, doc text is 'wid' (line from=0, to=3), and doc.sliceString(from, to) returns the text between the given offsets. Call the completion source and assert result.from === 0 (start of 'wid'), not 3 (cursor pos). Add second test with cursor mid-word after non-word chars: text='x = wid', pos=7, line.from=0 — expect from=4 (start of 'wid' not 'x').

E-11 impl: In completions.ts, before the return statement (line 112), compute the word start by scanning backward from pos. Get the line text via state.doc.sliceString(line.from, pos), iterate backward to find the first non-identifier char (not matching /[a-zA-Z0-9_]/), and set `const wordStart = line.from + i + 1` where i is the index of the last non-identifier char. Use wordStart as `from` in the returned CompletionResult instead of `pos`.

E-12 test: Add tests in gotoDefinition.test.ts. Test 1: 'calls onNavigate callback when definition is in a different file'. Create extension with onNavigate spy, simulate Ctrl+Click, mock invoke to return a location with a different URI. Assert onNavigate was called with (differentUri, line, character) and view.dispatch was NOT called. Test 2: 'same-file definition still dispatches cursor movement without calling onNavigate'. Same setup but location URI matches current URI. Assert view.dispatch was called and onNavigate was NOT called.

E-12 impl: Modify reifyGotoDefinition signature to accept optional onNavigate callback: reifyGotoDefinition(uri, onNavigate?: (targetUri: string, line: number, character: number) => void). In the mousedown handler, when location.uri differs from current URI: if onNavigate is provided, call onNavigate(location.uri, location.range.start.line, location.range.start.character); otherwise silently skip (preserving backward compatibility). In Editor.tsx, pass an onNavigate callback that: (a) extracts path from URI by stripping 'file://' prefix, (b) calls bridge.openFile(path) to load content, (c) calls props.store.openFile({path, content}) to open in store, (d) defers cursor navigation by dispatching to the view after the file switch effect runs (use setTimeout or Promise.resolve to yield to the reactive update).

E-14 test: Add test in hover.test.ts: 'tooltip create() returns DOM with cm-lsp-hover class and verifiable structure'. Call the hover handler to get a tooltip result, invoke the create() function on the returned tooltip, and assert that dom.className includes 'cm-lsp-hover'. This validates the CSS selector will match.

E-14 impl: In Editor.module.css, add :global(.cm-lsp-hover) CSS rule with background-color (dark theme: #2d2d3f or var(--reify-tooltip-bg)), color (#e0e0e0), border (1px solid #444), border-radius (4px), box-shadow (0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)), padding (6px 10px), max-width (480px), font-size (13px), white-space (pre-wrap), and line-height (1.4). Remove the redundant inline styles from hover.ts create() (padding, maxWidth, whiteSpace) since CSS now handles them — keep only className assignment and textContent.

E-12 integration test (REVIEW FIX): Add test in Editor.test.tsx in a new 'Editor cross-file goto-definition (E-12)' describe block. The test verifies that when a Ctrl+Click triggers a goto-definition response pointing to a different file, Editor.tsx actually navigates to that file via bridge.openFile and store.openFile. Setup: (1) render Editor with file1 active, (2) mock invoke to return a cross-file definition location (uri: 'file:///project/src/mount.ri', range.start: {line: 5, character: 2}) when textDocument/definition is requested, (3) spy on bridge.openFile to return file2's FileData, (4) get the EditorView and simulate Ctrl+Click via a MouseEvent on contentDOM with ctrlKey:true (use view.posAtCoords mock or directly dispatch mousedown on contentDOM). After awaiting the async chain (vi.waitFor), assert: (a) bridge.openFile was called with '/project/src/mount.ri' (the path extracted from the URI), (b) store.state.activeFile === file2.path (the store switched to the target file). This test will FAIL because Editor.tsx:73 currently passes no onNavigate callback.

E-12 integration impl (REVIEW FIX): Wire the onNavigate callback in Editor.tsx. (1) Add `openFile as bridgeOpenFile` to the import from '../bridge' (line 10, alongside updateSource and saveFile). (2) At line 73, change `reifyGotoDefinition(() => currentUri)` to `reifyGotoDefinition(() => currentUri, (targetUri, line, character) => { ... })`. (3) The callback body: (a) strip 'file://' prefix from targetUri to get the filesystem path — `const path = targetUri.replace('file://', '')`, (b) call `bridgeOpenFile(path).then(fileData => { ... })` to load the file from disk, (c) inside the .then: call `props.store.openFile(fileData)` to add/switch to the file in the store (this sets activeFile, which triggers the createEffect file-switch), (d) use `setTimeout(() => { if (view) { const targetLine = view.state.doc.line(line + 1); const targetPos = targetLine.from + character; view.dispatch({ selection: { anchor: targetPos }, scrollIntoView: true }); } }, 0)` to defer cursor navigation until after SolidJS's reactive file-switch effect has run and the EditorView has the new document. (4) Add `.catch((err: unknown) => console.error('Cross-file goto-definition error:', err))` for error handling on the bridgeOpenFile promise.

design_decisions.decision: Add @codemirror/search as a runtime dependency rather than using a custom search implementation
design_decisions.rationale: CodeMirror's official search package provides find/replace with regex support, keyboard shortcuts, and a styled panel out of the box. It's the standard approach and there's no benefit to building custom.

design_decisions.decision: Scan backward for word start in completions.ts using /[a-zA-Z0-9_]/ character class
design_decisions.rationale: Reify identifiers consist of alphanumeric chars and underscores. Scanning backward from cursor to find where the identifier starts ensures completion.from is correct, preventing prefix duplication when accepting a completion. This is the standard technique used by LSP-powered completion sources.

design_decisions.decision: Add onNavigate callback to reifyGotoDefinition rather than passing editorStore directly
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps gotoDefinition.ts decoupled from the store and bridge modules. The Editor.tsx component is the natural integration point where store + bridge access exists. The callback pattern matches how the existing code handles URI resolution (function getters). Making the callback optional preserves backward compatibility.

design_decisions.decision: Put hover tooltip CSS in Editor.module.css using :global() selector
design_decisions.rationale: Editor.module.css already uses :global() for .cm-editor and .cm-scroller overrides. CodeMirror tooltip containers are appended inside the editor DOM tree, so :global() scoping from the editor container is the correct and consistent approach.

design_decisions.decision: Use bridge.openFile for cross-file goto-definition to load file content from disk
design_decisions.rationale: bridge.openFile(path) already exists (bridge.ts line 53) and returns FileData with path+content. The editorStore.openFile(fileData) expects FileData. This is the natural integration path — no new bridge commands needed.

design_decisions.decision: closeBrackets extension added alongside closeBracketsKeymap for proper keyboard handling
design_decisions.rationale: closeBrackets() provides auto-insertion behavior, but closeBracketsKeymap adds the Backspace handler that deletes both brackets when cursor is between a pair. Both are needed for a correct UX. They are both exported from @codemirror/autocomplete which is already installed.

design_decisions.decision: Use setTimeout/microtask to defer cursor navigation after cross-file goto-definition
design_decisions.rationale: When onNavigate opens a new file via the store, the Editor's reactive file-switch effect runs asynchronously via SolidJS createEffect. The cursor navigation to the target definition position must happen AFTER the new EditorState is set. A setTimeout(0) or Promise.resolve().then() yields to the reactive update before dispatching the cursor movement.

design_decisions.decision: Import bridge.openFile as bridgeOpenFile alias in Editor.tsx
design_decisions.rationale: Editor.tsx already uses the name 'openFile' from editorStore (via props.store.openFile). Using the alias 'bridgeOpenFile' avoids name collision and makes it clear at each call site whether we're loading from disk (bridge) or updating the store (store.openFile). This follows the aliasing pattern already used in App.tsx line 28.

reuse.how: Reuse for all new Editor.test.tsx tests to get the CM6 view instance from rendered container

reuse.what: setupStore() helper and file fixtures (file1, file2, file3)
reuse.how: Reuse for new Editor extension tests

reuse.what: Tauri API mock pattern (vi.mock for @tauri-apps/api/core and event)
reuse.where: All existing test files (Editor.test.tsx, completions.test.ts, gotoDefinition.test.ts, hover.test.ts)
reuse.how: Follow the same mock pattern consistently in all test additions

reuse.what: bridge.openFile(path) function for loading files from disk
reuse.how: Call from Editor.tsx's onNavigate callback to load file content for cross-file goto-definition
Use in cross-file goto-definition callback to open/switch to the target file
Create inverse uriToPath() helper for converting LSP file:// URIs back to filesystem paths for bridge calls
Use same :global() pattern for .cm-lsp-hover tooltip styling

reuse.what: Existing CompletionContext mock shape in completions.test.ts
reuse.how: Extend with sliceString method on doc mock for word-boundary scanning tests
Extend the ext/mousedownHandler pattern to test onNavigate callback for cross-file definitions

reuse.what: mockInvoke pattern from Editor.test.tsx LSP serialization tests
reuse.how: Reuse the mockInvoke.mockImplementation pattern that intercepts lsp_request calls and routes by method, for the E-12 integration test to mock textDocument/definition responses
vi.spyOn(bridge, 'openFile') to mock the bridge call in the E-12 integration test and return controlled FileData
